page: Microsoft Azure E64_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 432.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.75 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1638 GB of local storage. No GPUs are bundled with this configuration. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates a clear division between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier, as do single-threaded compression and decompression tasks. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core scores landing in the middle 50% bracket. Notably, large-block uncached memory bandwidth is strong, placing in the top 25% for both read and write operations, while small-block cached read bandwidth remains average.

Given its high memory capacity and local storage, the E64_v3 is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing. It achieves average throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, it is poorly suited for LLM text generation, where it registers in the bottom 10% for both small and medium models. The hardware profile offers a high-density memory configuration, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for legacy enterprise applications and relational databases that require significant RAM but do not demand high single-core compute speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure E64_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 432.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization, it includes 1638 GB of local storage but lacks GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks reveal weak single-core CPU execution, placing in the bottom 25%, contrasted by average multi-core scaling and strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth in the top 25%. This resource profile makes the server qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy enterprise applications, database hosting, and multi-threaded processing tasks. It is less suitable for single-threaded operations or LLM text generation due to low single-core throughput.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 432.0 GB of RAM, and 1638 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v3 features 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 432.0 GB of RAM, and 1638 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated vCPUs and substantial local storage for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 432.0 GB of system memory
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Average multi-core performance for parallel workloads
Suited for database hosting and memory-intensive enterprise applications
